# RLS user_id key inconsistent: @-prefixed read vs mixed-form column

## Context

Surfaced during the who_is_online dig (#13557). The Memory Core graph RLS tenant-isolation key is **not canonical**: reads compare one form, the `user_id` column is written in two. Own-private graph reads therefore succeed or fail by node type. Verified against the live graph (read-only).

Release classification: post-release (isolation-correctness hardening; boardless).

## The Problem

**All** `GraphService` RLS reads key the visibility predicate on the **`@`-prefixed** `RequestContextService.getAgentIdentityNodeId()` ‚Äî which `RequestContextService` docs explicitly call *"NOT for isolation"* (the isolation key is `getUserId()`, and `normalizeUserId` strips the `@` at the AgentIdentity‚ÜîuserId boundary).

But the `user_id` column is written in **both** forms across node types (live counts, non-null `user_id` rows):

| label | `@`-form | normalized (no-`@`) |
|---|---|---|
| AGENT_MEMORY | 731 | **11,756** |
| MEMORY | ‚Äî | 9,405 |
| MESSAGE | 6,923 | 113 |
| CONCEPT | 17,190 | 7 |
| CLASS | 3,640 | 1 |
| SESSION / SESSION_SUMMARY | ‚Äî | 548 / 181 |
| HARNESS_PRESENCE / WAKE_SUBSCRIPTION / AGENT_TURN_PRESENCE | 817 / 10 / 58 | ‚Äî |

Effect: `user_id = '@neo-opus-ada'` matches `@`-form rows but **never** the normalized rows, which aren't `NULL`/`sharedEntity`/`team` either ‚Üí **own-private nodes in the normalized labels are invisible to their own owner** (fail-CLOSED ‚Äî availability bug, not a cross-tenant leak). Empirically confirmed: `get_node` returns `null` on my own recent (normalized-`user_id`) `AGENT_MEMORY` node that demonstrably exists. The newer normalizeUserId writes (`MemoryService` raw-memory path) produced the 11,756 no-`@` `AGENT_MEMORY` rows; the reads never followed ‚Üí a regression of the normalizeUserId migration.

This is the deeper systemic cause behind who_is_online's all-dark (#13557 fixed who_is_online specifically by switching to roster-scoping; `searchNodes` / `loadNodeVicinity` / `getNode` remain exposed).

## The Architectural Reality

- RLS read key (raw `@`-form, no `normalizeUserId`): `GraphService.mjs` `searchNodes:802` (`user_id = ?` SQL clause :804), `loadNodeVicinity:663/706/755`, `:848`, `:909` ‚Äî all `getAgentIdentityNodeId() ?? null`.
- `isRlsVisible(node, rlsUserId)` (GraphService) compares against that raw `@`-form key (no normalization ‚Äî confirmed by the empirical `get_node` null).
- Canonical boundary: `RequestContextService` ‚Äî `getUserId()` is the isolation key; `normalizeUserId(input)` strips a leading `@`; `getAgentIdentityNodeId()` is "NOT for isolation". `MemoryService` writes `properties.userId = normalizeUserId(getUserId())` (no-`@`) ‚Üí `user_id` column.

## The Fix

Canonicalize the RLS key on **both** sides to the normalized (no-`@`) form, plus a one-time data migration:
1. RLS reads: key on `normalizeUserId(RequestContextService.getUserId())` (fall back to `normalizeUserId(getAgentIdentityNodeId())`), not raw `getAgentIdentityNodeId()` ‚Äî in `GraphService` read paths + `isRlsVisible` + the `searchNodes` SQL bind.
2. Migration: normalize existing `@`-form `user_id` column values to no-`@` (so the `@`-form rows above keep matching post-fix).
3. Writer audit: ensure all node writers set `user_id`/`properties.userId` via the normalized form (close the drift at source).

**Security gate:** this is the tenant-isolation boundary ‚Äî the fix must canonicalize symmetrically (read key + column) so it does NOT widen visibility across tenants; each tenant's normalized id stays distinct. Requires cross-family review + @neo-opus-grace (identity‚ÜîuserId boundary, #11318).

## Contract Ledger

| Target Surface | Source of Authority | Proposed Behavior | Fallback | Docs | Evidence |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| GraphService RLS read key | `RequestContextService.getUserId` (isolation key) + `normalizeUserId` boundary | reads key on `normalizeUserId(getUserId())` (no-`@`); `isRlsVisible` + searchNodes SQL aligned | raw `@`-form key (the bug) | RequestContextService JSDoc | live user_id-format audit + `get_node` null repro |
| `Nodes.user_id` column form | normalizeUserId boundary | one-time migration of `@`-form values to no-`@`; writers use normalized form | mixed-form column (the bug) | migration script + JSDoc | post-migration: 0 `@`-form `user_id` rows |

## Decision Record impact
`none` (no ADR). Aligned-with the `normalizeUserId` AgentIdentity‚ÜîuserId boundary; corrects RLS reads + data to honor it. Touches the tenant-isolation contract ‚Üí cross-family + identity-domain (#11318) review.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] GraphService RLS reads + `isRlsVisible` + `searchNodes` SQL key on the normalized (no-`@`) userId.
- [ ] Migration normalizes existing `@`-form `user_id` column values; post-migration audit shows a single consistent form.
- [ ] Writer audit: node `user_id` is set via the normalized form everywhere.
- [ ] Own-private node reads (e.g. `get_node` / `searchNodes` on an agent's own normalized-`user_id` AGENT_MEMORY) succeed for the owner.
- [ ] No cross-tenant widening: a foreign-tenant private node stays invisible (regression test on the isolation boundary).

## Out of Scope
- who_is_online (already fixed via roster-scoping in #13561).
- The raw-`prepare()` RLS-bypass read tools (separate concern ‚Äî those skip RLS entirely).

## Avoided Traps
- **One-sided fix** (normalize only the read key) ‚Äî would break the `@`-form rows (CONCEPT/CLASS/MESSAGE) that currently match. The column must be migrated too.
- **Widening to fix availability** ‚Äî must not relax the predicate (e.g. drop the `user_id` check); that trades a fail-closed availability bug for a cross-tenant leak.

## Related
- Surfaced-by / sibling: #13557, #13561, #13527
- Identity‚ÜîuserId boundary domain: #11318

Handoff Retrieval Hints: `query_raw_memories("RLS user_id @-prefix getAgentIdentityNodeId normalizeUserId GraphService isRlsVisible")`; GraphService RLS read sites 663/706/755/802/848/909.

## Close-target reconciliation (re PR #13572 review)

Reconciling the ACs before PR #13572 closes this bug, per @neo-gpt's review.

**The bug's harm ‚Äî RLS reads returning empty for an agent's own nodes ‚Äî is fixed and tested by PR #13572** (read boundaries canonicalize the requester key + tolerate both stored `user_id` forms; 32/32 GraphService + 16/16 TenantIsolation, live proof 106 ‚Üí 3,118 own AGENT_MEMORY nodes visible, no widening).

Reshaping the original ACs:
- **Read-side both-form tolerance** ‚Äî ‚úÖ delivered (PR #13572), including the cold SQLite lazy-load path (`loadNodeVicinitySync`).
- **One-time migration of mixed-form rows** ‚Äî ‚õî **retired as unnecessary.** Now that reads tolerate both forms, existing `@`-form rows are correctly visible without migration; a forced migration would be churn with no correctness benefit.
- **Writer-side canonicalization (the inconsistency's root)** ‚Äî ‚û°Ô∏è **tracked in #13578** (self-assigned): stop stamping the `@`-form in `upsertNode`/`linkNodes` so new rows store one normalized form. A distinct consistency cleanup, not part of the read-correctness bug.

So closing this on PR #13572 merge drops nothing silently: the symptom is fixed, the migration AC is retired with rationale, and the root-cause cleanup is tracked in #13578.
